top_signals.title: Kuwait Says Oil Output Won't Recover for 10-12 Weeks After Hormuz Reopens
top_signals.summary: Kuwait Petroleum Company expects it will take considerably longer to restore oil production than many traders appear to assume if the Strait of Hormuz reopens in the coming days. Speaking at the S&P Global Energy Middle East Petroleum and Gas Conference, the company’s managing director for international marketing, Shaikh Khaled Ahmad Al-Sabah, said Kuwait would need six to eight weeks to recover roughly 70% of normal production levels after Hormuz reopens, with the remaining 30% requiring about another month.  Refining operations…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: SpaceX targets record $75 billion IPO as bitcoin treasury and liquidity risks draw focus
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Nomura's Nanavati on Asia M&A Momentum, Strategy
top_signals.summary: Nomura Head of Investment Banking for Asia ex-Japan Jwalant Nanavati shares his perspective on how landmark listings like SpaceX could shape the appetite for mergers and acquisitions across Asia and beyond. He speaks with Haslinda Amin from the sidelines of 'Nomura Investment Forum Asia' in Singapore. (Source: Bloomberg)
